Subject: Math Prerequisites
Title: Mathematical Methods in the Physical Sciences
Notes: Start here for undergraduates. Boas more readable than Arfken. Use Arfken for reference once comfortable.

Subject: Math Prerequisites
Title: Mathematical Methods for Physicists
Why Recommended: Comprehensive reference; standard in grad programs
Notes: Use as reference manual. Read chapters as needed. Very dense.

Subject: Math Prerequisites
Title: Calculus
Notes: Ensure competency in partial derivatives; Jacobians; Hessians.

Subject: Linear Algebra
Title: Introduction to Linear Algebra
Alternative/Supplement: Linear Algebra Done Right by Axler (more proof-focused)
Notes: MIT OCW 18.06 videos (free) + course materials. Essential for quantum mechanics.

Subject: ODE/PDE
Title: Partial Differential Equations for Scientists & Engineers
Notes: Focus on separation of variables; wave equation; heat equation.

Subject: Complex Analysis
Title: Complex Analysis (Princeton Lectures)
Notes: Essential for residue theorem. Ahlfors is terse; Boas more accessible first.

Subject: Classical Mechanics
Title: An Introduction to Mechanics
Notes: Best undergraduate starting point. Problems range from computational to conceptual.

Subject: Classical Mechanics
Title: Classical Mechanics
Notes: Spend Year 1-2 on this. Chapters 8-9 (Hamilton-Jacobi) deferred to advanced.

Subject: Classical Mechanics
Title: Mechanics (Volume 1)
Notes: Not an introductory text. Return to after Goldstein. Emphasizes principles; few worked examples.

Subject: Classical Mechanics
Title: Mathematical Methods of Classical Mechanics
Notes: For mathematically sophisticated learners. Reveals deep structure of mechanics.

Subject: Electromagnetism
Title: Introduction to Electrodynamics
Why Recommended: Informal tone; physically intuitive; excellent figures
Notes: PRIMARY TEXT. Now published by Cambridge (cheaper than Pearson). Edition 5 updated.

Subject: Electromagnetism
Title: Classical Electrodynamics
Notes: Standard graduate reference. Requires patience. Use after Griffiths mastery.

Subject: Electromagnetism
Title: The Feynman Lectures on Physics Vol. II
Notes: Supplement Griffiths for intuition. Not comprehensive but deeply insightful.

Subject: Electromagnetism
Title: Electricity and Magnetism (Berkeley Physics Course)
Notes: Out of print but available used. SI units; gem of pedagogy.

Subject: Quantum Mechanics
Title: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
Notes: PRIMARY TEXT. Moved from Prentice Hall to Cambridge. Excellent pedagogy.

Subject: Quantum Mechanics
Title: Quantum Mechanics: The Theoretical Minimum
Notes: Free lecture notes from University of Cambridge. Complements Griffiths.

Subject: Quantum Mechanics
Title: Quantum Mechanics Volumes I II & III
Notes: Definitive graduate reference. Expensive but worth it for serious students. French edition reprinted 2020.

Subject: Quantum Mechanics
Title: Quantum Mechanics
Notes: NOT an introduction. Return after graduate-level study. Reveals principles beneath formalism.

Subject: Quantum Mechanics
Title: Modern Quantum Mechanics
Notes: Recently revised (2021). Emphasizes symmetries and group theory early. Excellent.

Subject: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
Title: Fundamentals of Statistical and Thermal Physics
Why Recommended: Exceptionally clear; conceptual depth; excellent problems
Notes: BEST UNDERGRAD TEXT. Despite 1965 publication; clarity unsurpassed. Still in print.

Subject: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
Title: An Introduction to Thermal Physics
Notes: Modern alternative to Reif. Less rigorous but highly accessible. Good for engineering context.

Subject: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
Title: Statistical Physics Part 1 (Vol. 5)
Notes: Graduate reference. Not a primary text but excellent for deep understanding.

Subject: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
Title: Statistical Mechanics
Notes: Standard modern graduate text. Good balance of rigor and readability.

Subject: Thermodynamics & Statistical Mechanics
Title: Molecular Driving Forces
Notes: Excel at building intuition. Physics through biology applications.

Subject: Optics & Photonics
Title: Principles of Optics (Born & Wolf)
Alternative/Supplement: Hecht (more accessible); Griffiths Ch. 9 (EM waves intro)
Notes: The definitive optics reference. Very mathematical. Start with Hecht.

Subject: Optics & Photonics
Title: Optics
Notes: Excellent undergraduate text. Recently updated (5th ed). Great figures.

Subject: Optics & Photonics
Title: Physics of Light and Optics
Why Recommended: Free online; covers geometric and physical optics
Notes: High-quality open-source curriculum from Brigham Young University.

Subject: Condensed Matter / Solid State
Title: Introduction to Solid State Physics
Notes: Updated 9th edition (2018). Best starting point for undergraduates.

Subject: Condensed Matter / Solid State
Title: Solid State Physics
Notes: The graduate bible of condensed matter. Still standard despite 1976 publication.

Subject: Condensed Matter / Solid State
Title: Modern Condensed Matter Physics
Notes: Recently published. Reflects contemporary condensed matter research directions.

Subject: Fluid Mechanics
Title: Fluid Mechanics (Volume 6)
Notes: Landau's swan song. Comprehensive but assumes maturity. Physical insight throughout.

Subject: Fluid Mechanics
Title: Fluid Mechanics (6th ed)
Notes: Modern alternative. Well-illustrated; more engineering applications.

Subject: Special Relativity
Title: Introduction to Special Relativity
Notes: Excellent grounding in Minkowski geometry. Prepares for general relativity.

Subject: Special Relativity
Title: Spacetime and Geometry
Notes: Free lecture notes. Concise. Best starting point before tackling full GR.

Subject: General Relativity
Title: Lecture Notes on General Relativity
Notes: Available free from arXiv (gr-qc/9712019). Best for 1-semester intro.

Subject: General Relativity
Title: A First Course in General Relativity
Notes: Excellent for first exposure. Builds intuition before abstract formalism.

Subject: General Relativity
Title: General Relativity
Notes: The standard graduate reference. Dense but precise.

Subject: General Relativity
Title: Gravitation
Notes: MTW is massive and expensive but contains everything. Use as reference. Not for sequential reading.

Subject: Quantum Field Theory
Title: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory
Notes: THE standard QFT textbook. Comprehensive. Most graduate programs use this.

Subject: Quantum Field Theory
Title: Quantum Field Theory
Notes: Well-written alternative. More accessible in places than P&S.

Subject: Quantum Field Theory
Title: Quantum Field Theory as Simply as Possible
Notes: Best for conceptual understanding before diving into Peskin.

Subject: Quantum Field Theory
Title: Quantum Mechanics & Quantum Field Theory
Why Recommended: Clear exposition; good on path integrals
Notes: Out of print but available used. Underrated. Excellent on fundamentals.

Subject: Computational Physics
Title: Computational Physics
Alternative/Supplement: Numerical Recipes in C++ (comprehensive but less modern)
Notes: Paired with Python. Free online. Excellent for integrating numerics with physics.

Subject: Computational Physics
Title: Numerical Recipes (3rd ed)
Why Recommended: Algorithms; implementations in C++; standard reference
Notes: Use alongside SciPy for Python. Algorithms are language-independent.

Subject: Computational Physics
Title: Numerical Methods in Physics with Python
Notes: Recent (2020). Excellent balance of theory and implementation.

Subject: Mathematical Methods
Title: Tensors and Relativity
Alternative/Supplement: Arfken Ch. 2 (more abstract); MTW Ch. 8-9 (in context of GR)
Notes: Good bridge between vector calculus and tensor formalism.

Subject: Group Theory
Title: Group Theory in a Nutshell for Physicists
Notes: Excellent introduction. Zee's trademark accessible style.

Subject: Group Theory
Title: Lie Algebras in Particle Physics
Notes: Standard for particle physics community. Dense but rewarding.

Subject: Problem Books / Supplements
Title: 200 Puzzling Physics Problems
Alternative/Supplement: Irodov (classic but older); Kleppner & Kolenkow problem set
Notes: Excellent for deepening problem-solving skills beyond textbook exercises.

Subject: Problem Books / Supplements
Title: Problems in General Physics
Alternative/Supplement: Kleppner & Kolenkow; Any modern textbook problem set
Notes: Older (1988) but extensive. Good for drilling standard problem types.

Subject: Problem Books / Supplements
Title: The Feynman Lectures on Physics (all 3 volumes)
Why Recommended: Unparalleled physical intuition; 60 years of insights
Notes: Supplement primary texts. Feynman's perspective invaluable.

Subject: Laboratory / Experimental
Title: An Introduction to Error Analysis
Why Recommended: Best-practice error analysis; essential for labs
Notes: STANDARD undergraduate lab text. Every physics student should read this.

Subject: Laboratory / Experimental
Title: Data Reduction and Error Analysis
Notes: Modern (4th ed 2020). Includes Python examples. Better for advanced undergrads/grads.

Subject: Laboratory / Experimental
Title: Measurements and their Uncertainties
Notes: Good for experiments in quantum and solid state contexts.

Subject: Online Open Source
Title: MIT OpenCourseWare Physics Courses
Why Recommended: Free access to MIT physics curriculum; full lecture notes + exams
Notes: Exceptional resource. Use to supplement any topic.

Subject: Online Open Source
Title: Physics Stack Exchange & PhysicsForums
Why Recommended: Peer Q&A on specific topics; searchable
Notes: Use for clarification of concepts; check multiple answers.
